title: Gas Hydrate System Offshore Chile
abstract: The Chilean continental margin has been extensively investigated to characterize gas hydrate systems through seismic data analysis. A representative seismic line (RC2901-727) at ~36°S shows a strong and continuous BSR. High concentrations of hydrate ranging from 10% to 17% of total volume are recognized along the seismic lines. The BSR is not present where intensive deformations with faults and fractures dominate.

location.description: Full Chilean continental margin from ~33°S (Valparaíso) to ~57°S (Patagonia/Fuegian region); review of gas hydrate systems using SO161 and RC2901/RC2902 seismic lines

seismic_lines.name: RC2901-727 — representative MCS line at ~36°S (R/V Conrad 1988)
SO161 profiles — SPOC cruise at 36°–42°S (R/V SONNE 2001)

data.name: Compilation of MCS Lines — Chilean Margin Gas Hydrate System (~36°S to ~47°S)
data.description: Review of multiple MCS reflection lines (including representative line RC2901-727 at ~36°S) from the Chilean margin used to characterize gas hydrate BSR distribution and concentration from 36°–47°S; hydrate concentrations 10–17% total volume. [R/V Conrad RC2901 (Lamont, early 1990s) — no confirmed public data portal found]

data.name: Navigation:Primary [RC2901]
data.description: acquired during the Robert D. Conrad expedition RC2901 (1988)

_data_note: Open access MDPI Energies review. Uses compiled data from multiple cruises including R/V Conrad RC2901. RC2901 data accessible via MGDS.

processing.summary: Review paper compiling velocity analysis results from multiple MCS seismic lines along the Chilean margin (36°–47°S) for gas hydrate system characterization. The representative RC2901-727 line at ~36°S shows a strong, continuous BSR with hydrate concentrations 10–17% of total volume. BSR discontinuities correlate with tectonic faulting and fracturing in the accretionary prism.
workflow: 1. Compilation of published seismic data from multiple cruises
4. Gas hydrate concentration estimation using effective medium theory
processing.notes: Review/synthesis paper; MDPI open access. SO161 (SPOC): 3000m streamer, 132-channel (12.5m intertrace for first 24 ch, 25m rest), 20 air guns, 54.1L = ~3302 cui, 50m shot spacing. RC2901/RC2902 (Conrad 1988): 3300m digital streamer, 240 channels, 12.5m spacing, 10 air guns, 61.3L = ~3740 cui, 50m shot spacing. Paper covers full Chilean margin from Valparaíso to Patagonia/Fuegian Triple Junction area.

analysis_notes: Validated against PDF. CRITICAL: authors completely wrong (was Tinivella,Umberta+2) → 5 authors: Vargas-Cordero Ivan (1st), Giustiniani Michela (2nd), Tinivella Umberta (3rd), Villar-Muñoz Lucia (4th), Alessandrini Giulia (5th). vessel: added cruise IDs SO161/RC2901/RC2902. year_acquired: null → 1988 (RC2901/RC2902). streamer_length_m: null → 3000 (SO161). channel_count: null → 132 (SO161; RC2901/RC2902 has 240 ch, 3300m). source_volume_cui: SO161=54.1L=3302cui, RC2901=61.3L=3740cui. sample_rate_ms: 4 → null (not confirmed). software: 'unknown' → Kirchhoff PSDM. bounding_box expanded to -57 to -33 (Valparaíso to Fuegian region). seismic_lines: added SO161 profiles.
